title: Legacy DPI mode call
body:
Hi,

currently verilator requires `DPI-C` when importing DPI functions. When trying to use `DPI`
that works with VCS OK, we I get:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: dpi.sv:4:5: Unsupported DPI type 'DPI': Use 'DPI-C' (IEEE 1800-2023 35.5.4)
    4 |     import "DPI" function int get_value();
      |     ^~~~~~
```

According to Dave in:
https://electronics.stackexchange.com/questions/223518/would-you-let-me-know-how-to-use-the-dpi-and-dpi-c

the `DPI` is "old" before SV was standardized. Couldn't Verilator just use both and use
the same encoding / memory layout in both cases ?

I know this is simple to update in TB, but still it would be nice to support it without the need to update test-benches.
